author: @kelvin1316
text: Wow lots of videos in one go, thanks for clogging up my "subscribed" feed!

author: @ashtreegarden5079
text: once I made a little trebuchet for Christmas for my wife's brother's kids. It had a swingarm of approx 50cm and I made three wooden balls on a lathe for ammo. It happened to be so effective that the balls went straight throug the room when fired without actually loosing any elevation and they left a dent in the brick wall, so the kids were actually never allowed to play with it. But my wife's brother was totally amused and kept it in his office for retorsion purposes...

author: @michaelsams6873
text: I can see a couple of kids at the supper table with their plate of vegetables suddenly becoming fun. 😊😊

author: @ianelley
text: A " Frostner" bit ? I know we are in winter but I'm sure you meant a " Forstner " bit !! I used to think the same until I got told off by a very old chippy in the workshop I worked in ! Just glad to know I am not the only one ! lol.  Keep up the good work !!

author: @martinwinfield2935
text: Great idea thanks. I spotted the finished wood items and have used them. They are so much cheaper.

text: Great tip taping the wood together to drill it. :)

author: @dexterconnolly3696
text: That's so cool rob. I might make a slightly bigger one as a ball thrower for my dog

author: @stevetobias4890
text:
Have you ever thought of looking in the offcuts bin at your local hardware store. The wood prices range from free to very cheap.
Also don't mean to be pedantic but that drill piece is not called a "Frostner bit" it's a "Forstner bit", sorry don't like being picky, I know everyone knows what you meant. Great project!

author: @YoureNowOnTV
text:
Nice one sir! Thank you for inspiring us to create. With practice you’ll be mounting deadly accurate attacks on Luke in no time 😁👍
Btw, not that it matters but I think it’s a Forstner bit, not Frostner bit. They are cool though. 😊
